body.text: What was supposed to be a fresh start and happy ending, ended up being anything but.
The divorce was official only 6 months after we arrived.
The wings had fallen off of the aircraft.
My ex didn't want to share in raising his kid — despite me not only allowing him a chance, but encouraging it.
I really wanted to give my kid some sense of a normal two parent life — but it wasn't meant to be.
He took a job out of state. The in-laws ended in divorce, too.
I was ready to move out of state to start fresh.
My father-in-law was supposed to help me load up my moving truck.
That day came and I waited for him. And waited.
I drove to his house. Knocked.
I fell to my knees and started balling.
I stayed for an extra week to plan and attend his funeral.
But leaving the gloomy, gray sky of Pennsylvania — both literally and metaphorically — was the only thing left to do.
